ipos.summary: First Tata Group IPO in nearly two decades; an engineering R&D and digital services provider to global automotive and aerospace OEMs. Listed at a 140% premium.

overview.introduction: Tata Technologies is a global engineering services company offering product development and digital solutions, including turnkey solutions, to global original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and their tier-1 suppliers.
overview.businessModel: Revenue comes from Services (outsourced engineering and digital transformation) and Technology Solutions (products and education). Deep anchor relationships with Tata Motors and JLR provide a stable base, complemented by a growing roster of non-Tata global clients.
overview.industryAnalysis: The global engineering, research and development (ER&D) outsourcing market is expanding as automakers shift to electric and software-defined vehicles, pushing R&D spend toward external specialists.

news.title: Tata Technologies posts steady Q4; declares dividend
New deal signed with European OEM for EV platform
Stock completes one year since blockbuster listing

strengths: Anchor clients in Tata Motors and JLR
weaknesses: Client concentration in the Tata ecosystem
opportunities: Diversification into aerospace and industrial heavy machinery
threats: Slowdown in global auto R&D budgets
Intense competition from larger ER&D peers

ipos.summary: State-owned NBFC dedicated to financing renewable energy and efficiency projects. A multibagger after listing on the back of India's green energy push.

overview.introduction: IREDA is a Mini Ratna (Category-I) government enterprise under the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy, providing financial assistance to renewable energy and energy-efficiency projects.
overview.businessModel: Lends to solar, wind, hydro, and energy-efficiency projects, earning a net interest spread. Benefits from sovereign backing, access to low-cost funds, and priority-sector positioning.
overview.industryAnalysis: India targets 500 GW of non-fossil capacity by 2030, creating a vast financing runway for specialised green lenders.

news.title: Board approves QIP to fund loan-book growth

strengths: Sovereign ownership and low funding cost
weaknesses: Asset quality sensitive to project execution
opportunities: Green bonds and international climate finance
threats: Policy or tariff changes affecting RE viability

ipos.summary: One of India's largest domestic-focused pharma companies, known for mass-market brands. A steady post-listing compounder.

overview.introduction: Mankind Pharma develops, manufactures and markets pharmaceutical formulations and consumer healthcare products, with a strong presence in the domestic chronic and acute therapy markets.
overview.businessModel: Primarily domestic prescription and OTC sales through an extensive field force, with affordable mass-market brands driving volume-led growth.
overview.industryAnalysis: The Indian pharma market is structurally growing on rising incomes, insurance penetration and chronic-disease prevalence.

news.title: Mankind reports double-digit revenue growth in FY25
Completes acquisition to enter premium chronic segment

strengths: Healthy margins and low debt historically

ipos.summary: India's second-largest commercial port operator, part of the JSW Group, with a fast-growing cargo handling network.

overview.introduction: JSW Infrastructure operates ports and port terminals handling diverse cargo, providing maritime-related services across India's coastline.
overview.businessModel: Earns tariff-based revenue on cargo throughput, anchored by JSW Group volumes (steel, energy) with a growing third-party customer mix.
overview.industryAnalysis: Rising EXIM trade and the Sagarmala programme support long-term growth in private port capacity.

news.title: JSW Infra crosses record annual cargo volume
Expands terminal capacity at east-coast port
